summary: Fase 2 Bookvia (Wallet/Saldo del cliente) - 15/15 backend tests PASSED. Covers GET /me/wallet & /me/wallet/transactions, services.wallet (credit/debit/expire_stale_balances), POST /bookings/{id}/cancel/user with refund_to=wallet|card (>24h, <24h policies), and POST /payments/deposit/checkout with use_wallet=true (zero balance, full coverage no Stripe, partial with Stripe remainder).

minor.issue: Response payload does NOT include wallet_applied/stripe_charge_amount fields (they are stored correctly in the transaction doc but not surfaced to the client). The wallet_only branch DOES return them. Consider adding wallet_applied and stripe_charge_amount to the standard response for FE consistency. File: /app/backend/routers/payments.py lines 375-384.

action_items: (Optional) Surface wallet_applied & stripe_charge_amount in /payments/deposit/checkout response when use_wallet=true and Stripe path is used (parity with wallet_only branch).
critical_code_review_comments: services/wallet.py is well-structured: explicit CREDIT_TYPES/DEBIT_TYPES whitelists prevent invalid tx types; atomic find_one_and_update with $gte balance guard prevents race-condition over-debit; expire_stale_balances delegates to debit_wallet so an audit trail is preserved.

Cancel /api/bookings/{id}/cancel/user with refund_to=wallet correctly skips Stripe refund and credits business_amount (91.5% of deposit) into wallet via credit_wallet(CREDIT_CANCELLATION); transaction.refund_to & wallet_credited persisted.
<24h cancellation correctly forces refund_to='card' even when user requested wallet (because refund_amount=0), preventing zero-amount wallet entries.

/payments/deposit/checkout wallet_only branch correctly: debits full client_paid (deposit + bookvia_fee), creates PAID transaction with stripe_session_id=None, marks booking CONFIRMED, posts ledger entries.

Partial wallet checkout: correctly persists wallet_applied + stripe_charge_amount in transaction, but does NOT debit wallet at checkout time (debit happens in webhook on confirmation, line 432-438 of payments.py). This is correct: avoids debiting wallet for an unfinished Stripe payment.
_compute_expires_at uses 30 days * months which is approximate (24*30=720 days vs ~730 actual). Acceptable for display but may drift ~10 days from a true 24-month anniversary.
test_credentials: User: testuser_stats@test.com / TestPass123! | Business: testbiz_dashboard@test.com / TestBiz123!
seed_data_creation: Inserted ephemeral TEST_F2_EXP service ($500), TEST_F2_WORKER (7-day schedule), and several TEST_F2_co_* bookings + paid transactions on biz_dd14ed093b51. All cleaned up via fixtures and explicit deletes after each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Fase 2 wallet fully validated end-to-end. Reusable test file at /app/backend/tests/test_fase2_wallet.py (15 tests). The reset_wallet autouse fixture clears suspension/cancellation_count for testuser_stats@test.com before/after each test (necessary since cancel tests bump cancellation_count and may auto-suspend). Frontend skipped per request.
rca of the issue: N/A - no critical issues. Two initial test failures were due to over-asserting on response payload keys (wallet_applied/stripe_charge_amount) that the standard Stripe-path response doesn't expose; they ARE persisted to transaction doc. Adjusted tests to verify via DB.
